project.name: BRIDGE v2.2 Toolkit
project.description: Distributable methodology toolkit for AI-assisted software development. Provides structured workflows (Brainstorm → Requirements → Implementation Design → Develop → Gate → Evaluate) across multiple AI coding platforms. v2.2 keeps a shell-first installer surface and adds install-time personality overlays.

in_scope: 5 distributable packs: RooCode Full, RooCode Standalone, Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode
Advanced orchestrator packs: Controller and Multi-Repo
15 slash commands consistent across all packs
6 procedure skills per pack (adapted to pack architecture)
Unified shell installer (`bridge.sh`) for new, add, orchestrator, and pack workflows
Install-time personality overlays: strict, balanced, mentoring
README.md with installation and usage instructions
docs/ templates: requirements.json, context.json, decisions.md, human-playbook.md
non_goals: Replace the AI coding agent itself — BRIDGE is a methodology layer, not an agent
Support every possible AI coding tool — focus on RooCode, Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode
Ask users to choose domain specializations before brainstorming or requirements exist

technical: No runtime dependencies beyond tar, gzip, sed, grep, awk, find, and bash
All packs self-contained after bridge.sh new runs
{{PROJECT_NAME}} placeholder replaced at init time by the shell installer
bridge.sh is the only installer entrypoint; do not reintroduce wrapper scripts or a compiled installer
conventions: Stable IDs: Fxx (features), ATxx (acceptance tests), Sxx (slices), UFxx (user flows), Rxx (risks)
.roomodes uses YAML format with emoji mode names
All packs include Human Handoff Protocol (HUMAN: blocks)

F01.title: RooCode Full Pack
F01.description: .roo/ structure with .roomodes (YAML), rules/, rules-{mode}/, skills/, commands/, and docs/ templates. Thin commands that delegate to rules and skills.

F02.title: RooCode Standalone Pack
F02.description: .roo/commands/ with 15 self-contained command files embedding full prompts. No external rules or skills dependencies. docs/ templates included.

F03.title: Claude Code Pack
F03.description: CLAUDE.md (global rules + delegation model), .claude/agents/ (5 subagents), .claude/skills/ (6 procedures), .claude/commands/ (15 commands), .claude/hooks/README.md, docs/ templates.

F04.title: Codex Pack
F04.description: AGENTS.md (global rules + role modes), .agents/skills/ (15 user-invokable workflow skills), .agents/procedures/ (6 internal procedure docs referenced by workflow skills), .codex/config.toml, docs/ templates.

F05.title: Shell Installers
F05.description: bridge.sh covers new, add, orchestrator, and pack workflows as the single installer entrypoint.

F06.title: Methodology Reference Document
F06.description: reference/BRIDGE-v2.1-methodology.md — comprehensive methodology documentation covering all phases, schemas, and workflows.

F07.title: 15 Consistent Commands
F07.description: All packs implement the same 15 commands: brainstorm, scope, requirements, requirements-only, feature, design, start, resume, end, gate, eval, feedback, context-create, context-update, advisor.

F08.title: Existing Project Support
F08.description: bridge-scope (analyze existing codebase, scope feature/fix) and bridge-feature (incremental requirements appending to existing requirements.json).

F09.title: Post-Delivery Feedback Loop
F09.description: Orchestrator correctly classifies user responses after slice delivery: ISSUES REPORTED (default) → fix loop on current slice, APPROVED → proceed to gate/eval/next, STOP → wrap up.

F10.title: Packaging Script
F10.description: bridge.sh pack rebuilds all release tar.gz archives from source folders, including core packs and advanced orchestrator packs.

F11.title: OpenCode Pack
F11.description: AGENTS.md (global rules + delegation model), .opencode/agents/ (5 subagents), .opencode/skills/ (6 procedures), .opencode/commands/ (15 commands), docs/ templates.

F12.title: Design Integration Command
F12.description: bridge-design command for integrating a design document, PRD, or version spec into requirements. Present in all packs.

F13.title: Strategic Advisor Command
F13.description: bridge-advisor command providing honest multi-perspective review of project viability, quality, positioning, community engagement, roadmap gaps, and launch readiness. Three simulated roles: Product Strategist, Developer Advocate, Critical Friend.

F14.title: Controller Pack
F14.description: Portfolio-level orchestrator for managing multiple BRIDGE projects. CLAUDE.md + .claude/commands/ (3 commands: bridge-status, bridge-init-project, bridge-sync) + .claude/rules/controller.md + docs/portfolio.json + reference/controller-guide.md.

F15.title: Multi-Repo Pack
F15.description: Cross-repo workspace orchestrator with two variants (claude-code and codex). 12 commands including 4 cross-repo specific (bridge-cross-design, bridge-cross-review, bridge-cross-sync, bridge-repo-status) plus adapted standard workflow commands.

F16.title: Install-Time Personality Overlays
F16.description: bridge.sh new/add accept `--personality strict|balanced|mentoring`. Non-default personalities inject marked vibe lines into orchestrator files, advisor/brainstorm prompts, and role definitions during install.

F17.title: Cross-Pack Personality Validation Coverage
F17.description: Automated shell/E2E coverage verifies personality overlays for Codex and OpenCode CLI packs in addition to the existing Claude Code path, so pack-specific marker insertion does not drift silently.

F18.title: Specialist Auto-Selection
F18.description: After brainstorming or requirements exist, BRIDGE recommends relevant specialist add-ons from a catalog and bridge-start / bridge-resume auto-load the ones that match the current slice context. Users are not asked to choose specialists during install.

F19.title: Pluggable Specialist Catalog
F19.description: Specialists are described by catalog metadata and loading rules so new specialties can be added later without hardcoding each one into the installer surface or selection logic.

acceptance_tests.AT01: bridge.sh new --pack full --name TestProject creates working project with .roo/ structure and {{PROJECT_NAME}} replaced
acceptance_tests.AT02: bridge.sh new --pack standalone --name TestProject creates project with self-contained commands
acceptance_tests.AT03: bridge.sh new --pack claude-code --name TestProject creates project with CLAUDE.md and .claude/ structure
acceptance_tests.AT04: bridge.sh new --pack codex --name TestProject creates project with AGENTS.md and .agents/ structure
acceptance_tests.AT05: All 15 commands present and functional in each pack
acceptance_tests.AT06: bridge-scope and bridge-feature commands work against an existing codebase with existing requirements.json
acceptance_tests.AT07: Orchestrator stays in current slice fix loop when user reports issues after slice delivery (does not ask about next slice)
acceptance_tests.AT08: Orchestrator advances to next slice only on explicit user approval
acceptance_tests.AT09: Post-delivery feedback loop present in: orchestrator rules, slice-plan skill, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, standalone start/resume commands
acceptance_tests.AT10: bridge.sh pack rebuilds all release tar.gz archives from source folders
acceptance_tests.AT11: bridge.sh new --pack opencode --name TestProject creates project with AGENTS.md and .opencode/ structure
acceptance_tests.AT12: bridge-controller pack contains CLAUDE.md, 3 commands (bridge-status, bridge-init-project, bridge-sync), controller rules, portfolio.json template, and reference guide
acceptance_tests.AT13: bridge-multi-repo pack contains both claude-code and codex variants, each with 12 commands/skills, multi-repo rules, workspace docs templates, and reference playbook
acceptance_tests.AT14: bridge.sh new --pack claude-code --personality strict --name TestProject injects strict personality lines into CLAUDE.md, bridge-architect.md, and bridge-brainstorm.md
acceptance_tests.AT15: bridge.sh add --pack claude-code --personality mentoring --target ./existing-dir preserves existing project files while injecting mentoring personality lines into installed BRIDGE files
acceptance_tests.AT16: bridge.sh new --pack codex --personality strict --name TestProject injects strict personality lines into AGENTS.md and the Codex brainstorm/procedure surfaces selected by the overlay engine
acceptance_tests.AT17: bridge.sh new --pack opencode --personality mentoring --name TestProject injects mentoring personality lines into AGENTS.md and at least one OpenCode role file
acceptance_tests.AT18: bridge-requirements recommends relevant specialist add-ons from a catalog after idea/requirements context exists, without asking for specialist choice during install
acceptance_tests.AT19: bridge-start and bridge-resume auto-load the specialists attached to the current slice/context and explain why they were selected
acceptance_tests.AT20: A new specialist can be added through catalog metadata and content files without changing bridge.sh or hardcoding installer-time selection logic

UF01.title: Greenfield Project
steps: brainstorm → requirements → start → (slice loop) → gate → eval → feedback

UF02.title: Existing Project Feature
steps: scope → feature → start → (slice loop) → gate → eval → feedback

UF04.title: Slice Fix Loop
steps: slice delivered → user reports issues → orchestrator fixes → re-presents → user approves → gate

UF05.title: Portfolio Management
steps: bridge-init-project (scaffold child) → bridge-status (portfolio view) → bridge-sync (pull state)

UF06.title: Cross-Repo Feature
steps: bridge-scope → bridge-cross-design → bridge-start → (cross-repo slices) → bridge-gate (two-phase) → bridge-cross-review

UF07.title: Personality-Aware Install
steps: bridge.sh new → choose pack → choose personality → install BRIDGE files with tone overlay

UF08.title: Adaptive Specialist Flow
steps: brainstorm or scope → requirements identify likely specialist add-ons → start/resume auto-load specialists for the active slice → human can confirm or override

R01.description: Orchestrator ignores fix feedback and advances to next slice
R01.mitigation: Post-Delivery Feedback Loop with explicit classification rules and ISSUES REPORTED as default

R02.description: Pack divergence — commands/skills drift out of sync across 5 packs
R02.mitigation: Single source of truth for command list; bridge.sh pack for consistent rebuilds

R03.description: Personality patching corrupts installed markdown files if marker insertion or replacement breaks structure
R03.mitigation: Marker-based personality blocks, targeted file list, and shell E2E coverage for strict + mentoring installs

R04.description: Personality overlay support drifts between Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode because only one pack path is exercised regularly
R04.mitigation: Add Codex and OpenCode personality checks to the shell E2E suite and eval validator, not just Claude Code

R05.description: Specialist auto-selection chooses irrelevant add-ons or misses needed ones, causing noisy or incomplete guidance
R05.mitigation: Require transparent recommendation rationale in requirements/start/resume flows and keep human override available

R06.description: A future pluggable specialist catalog becomes ad hoc or incompatible across packs
R06.mitigation: Define catalog metadata/manifest expectations and validation rules before adding external or optional specialist packs

recommended_slices.goal: Remove the abandoned Go CLI path and restore a shell-first installer surface with personality overlays
Expand personality overlay validation coverage to Codex and OpenCode CLI packs
Add post-requirements specialist recommendation and auto-selection to start/resume flows
Define a pluggable specialist catalog so future specialties can be added without hardcoded installer logic

open_questions: What specialist metadata should bridge-requirements persist so bridge-start and bridge-resume can auto-load the right add-ons deterministically?
How explicit should the human override be when auto-selection recommends or loads a specialist that may not fit the slice?
What manifest/catalog format should future pluggable specialists implement so new entries can be validated without hardcoding their names into selection logic?
